title: salix phylicifolia subsp. hegetschweileri
intro: Salix phylicifolia subsp. hegetschweileri, also known as Hegetschweiler's willow, is a subspecies of willow tree that belongs to the Salicaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are derived from its bark and leaves. The health benefits of Salix phylicifolia subsp. hegetschweileri include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions such as wounds and infections. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the bark and leaves in hot water to create a tea or decoction that can be used to treat various health issues.
